questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu is Parashurama counted as?
questions.explanation: Parashurama is the sixth avatar of Vishnu, coming before Rama and Krishna.

questions.q: What weapon gives Parashurama his name?
questions.explanation: Parashurama means Rama of the axe, after the parashu he carried.

questions.q: Which god granted Parashurama his famous axe?
questions.explanation: Shiva, pleased by his austerities, bestowed the great axe upon Parashurama.

questions.q: Who was Parashurama's father?
questions.explanation: Parashurama was the youngest son of the sage Jamadagni.

questions.q: Who was Parashurama's mother?
questions.explanation: Renuka, wife of Jamadagni, was the mother of Parashurama.

questions.q: Which warrior class did Parashurama exterminate twenty-one times?
questions.explanation: In vengeance for his father, Parashurama cleansed the earth of kshatriyas twenty-one times.

questions.q: Which three Mahabharata warriors studied the art of war under Parashurama?
questions.explanation: After retiring from slaughter, Parashurama taught weapons to Bhishma, Drona, and Karna.

questions.q: Whom did Parashurama curse to forget his weapon lore at the crucial hour?
questions.explanation: Karna was cursed to forget his hard-won knowledge at the moment of greatest need.

questions.q: Why is Parashurama present across many ages of the epic world?
options: He is reborn in every age as a new sage
He visits the earth only when summoned
He is a chiranjivi, one of the deathless
He lives on through his Bhargava heirs
questions.explanation: Parashurama is counted among the chiranjivis, the immortals who endure through the ages.

questions.q: With which former pupil did Parashurama fight a twenty-three day duel?
questions.explanation: Parashurama battled his own student Bhishma for twenty-three days over the princess Amba.

questions.q: Why did Parashurama behead his own mother Renuka?
options: She had cursed his brothers unjustly
His father commanded it and he obeyed
She had abandoned the hermitage fire
A demon had taken possession of her form
questions.explanation: He alone obeyed Jamadagni's terrible command, then used the offered boon to restore his mother to life.

questions.q: Which king's seizure of the hermitage's sacred calf provoked Parashurama's wrath?
questions.explanation: The thousand-armed Kartavirya Arjuna carried off the calf of Jamadagni's sacred cow, beginning the feud.

questions.q: Where did Parashurama fill five lakes with kshatriya blood?
questions.explanation: At Samantapanchaka by Kurukshetra he filled five lakes with blood and offered libations to his ancestors.

questions.q: To whom did Parashurama gift the conquered earth?
questions.explanation: Having destroyed its kings, Parashurama gave the whole earth to the sage Kashyapa in sacrifice.

questions.q: On which mountain did Parashurama dwell when Karna sought him as guru?
questions.explanation: Parashurama retired to Mount Mahendra, where Karna came to learn the Brahmastra.

questions.q: To which great sage's lineage did Parashurama belong?
questions.explanation: Parashurama was a Bhargava, a descendant of the fiery sage Bhrigu.

questions.q: Why did Parashurama take up arms against his own student Bhishma?
options: Bhishma had insulted the Bhargava line
Bhishma refused to return a celestial weapon
Bhishma had broken his vow of celibacy
Bhishma refused to marry the princess Amba
questions.explanation: Championing the wronged Amba, Parashurama ordered Bhishma to marry her, and the refusal meant war.

questions.q: How did the long duel between Parashurama and Bhishma end?
options: Bhishma yielded out of respect for his guru
Parashurama struck Bhishma down on the final day
It ended in a standstill with neither defeated
The gods declared Parashurama the victor
questions.explanation: After twenty-three days the fight was stopped with neither beaten, and Parashurama admitted he could not defeat Bhishma.

questions.q: Why did Parashurama curse Karna?
options: Karna had posed as a brahmin to gain his teaching
Karna had stolen the Vijaya bow by night
Karna had wounded him in a practice bout
Karna had mocked the aged sage's strength
questions.explanation: Parashurama taught only brahmins, and Karna had hidden his kshatriya-like birth to become his student.

questions.q: How was Karna's true nature betrayed while Parashurama slept on his lap?
options: Indra appeared in a dream and revealed it
He silently endured an insect boring into his thigh
His natural golden armor gleamed through his robes
He instinctively reached for a sword in his sleep
questions.explanation: Only a kshatriya could bear such agony unmoved, the guru reasoned; retellings make the insect Indra in disguise.

questions.q: Which great bow did Parashurama bestow on Karna?
questions.explanation: Parashurama gave his finest student the Vijaya, the bow crafted by Vishvakarma.

questions.q: What exactly would Parashurama's curse do to Karna?
options: His bowstring would snap in every duel
His weapon lore would desert him at the hour of greatest need
His arrows would never strike a retreating foe
His chariot would sink whenever he advanced
questions.explanation: The curse made Karna forget the Brahmastra when he needed it most; the sinking wheel came from a separate brahmin's curse.

questions.q: Which cautionary tale did Parashurama tell at the Kuru court before the war?
options: The drowning of King Sagara's sons
The humbling of King Dambhodbhava by Nara and Narayana
questions.explanation: He warned Duryodhana with the tale of Dambhodbhava, hinting that Arjuna and Krishna were Nara and Narayana reborn.

questions.q: Who finally halted the duel between Parashurama and Bhishma?
options: Amba, who withdrew her claim in despair
Ganga, who rose between the fighters
His Bhargava ancestors with the sage Narada
questions.explanation: Parashurama's pitrs, joined by Narada and the gods, commanded him to lay down his arms.

questions.q: Which weapon was Bhishma about to loose at Parashurama when the duel was stopped?
questions.explanation: Bhishma was readying the Praswapa, a weapon of irresistible sleep revealed to him in a dream, when he was forbidden to use it.

questions.q: How did Parashurama's father Jamadagni die?
options: Slain by Kartavirya's sons in revenge
Burned in his own hermitage's fire
Struck down by a rival sage's curse
questions.explanation: The sons of Kartavirya murdered the sage Jamadagni, triggering Parashurama's vow against all kshatriyas.

questions.q: What did Parashurama give Drona, who came seeking wealth as the sage gave away all he owned?
options: The last of his Bhargava gold
His weapons along with their secret mantras
The axe he had carried since youth
A blessing of unmatched fame alone
questions.explanation: Having given everything else away, Parashurama offered his weapons and their secrets, and Drona gladly accepted.

questions.q: What did Parashurama tell Amba when his great duel with Bhishma ended?
options: That Bhishma would surely relent in time
That she should return to the king of Shalva
That he would raise an army in her cause
That he could do no more and she must find another way
questions.explanation: Admitting he could not defeat Bhishma, he left Amba to her austerities, which won her Shiva's promise of revenge.

questions.q: Who was Parashurama's grandfather in the Bhargava line?
questions.explanation: Richika, father of Jamadagni, was Parashurama's grandfather.

questions.q: Asked how he would duel Bhishma without a chariot, what did Parashurama reply?
options: That his axe needed no platform to kill
That Shiva would send him a celestial car
That he would fight on foot as brahmins do
That the earth was his chariot and the Vedas his steeds
questions.explanation: He declared the earth his chariot, the Vedas his horses, and the wind his charioteer.
